title: OpenTitan lc_ctrl -> KMAC CDC missing synchronizer
hook_30s: This bug shipped through RTL simulation and was found in gate-level simulation — hundreds of regression hours wasted. It's a clock-domain crossing inside OpenTitan's life-cycle controller, on the path to the cryptographic accelerator. ChipGPT finds it in 23 seconds, before GLS.

steps.agent: I'll check this RTL against its CDC specification. Let me start by extracting spec facts and parsing the RTL.
Reading the spec; extracting the canonicality rule.

Spec rule at line 9: every cross-domain signal must pass through a synchronizer. RTL has a sequential block clocked on `clk_main_i`. Let me run structural CDC analysis (this is the only tool that can find this — sim cannot) and locate the crossing.

In lc_ctrl_kmac_if, lc_token_i is generated in the clk_io_div4 domain but is captured directly by a single flop (token_q) clocked on clk_main_i, with no two-flop synchronizer or CDC handshake on the io_div4 -> main crossing. Synchronizer depth = 1 (direct capture) where the spec requires depth >= 2 (prim_flop_2sync) for level/single-bit, or a full handshake for multi-bit data.

artifact.explanation: The property asserts the destination output equals a 2-flop synchronized version of the source-domain input, as required by the CDC methodology. Formal (SymbiYosys/z3, BMC depth 4) returns CEX: the buggy DUT produces the single-flop value, violating the spec-mandated synchronizer depth.
artifact.result: SymbiYosys (z3): counterexample found — bug proven formally

steps.agent: Running the directed test in the simulator.

artifact.name: tb_lc_kmac_cdc_depth
artifact.stimulus: Reset, then change lc_token_i to 0xA5 and sample kmac_token_o one clk_main_i edge later, comparing against a reference 2-flop synchronizer (s2).
artifact.result_on_buggy: PASS in zero-delay sim (dut=a5, spec_2flop_sync=a5, raw=a5) — sim cannot see the missing synchronizer, exactly as the spec warns. Caught instead by cdc_report (violation, line 20) and formal (CEX).
artifact.result_on_fixed: PASS — with prim_flop_2sync inserted, output equals the synchronized reference and cdc_report reports no violation.

artifact.title: Unsynchronized clock-domain crossing: lc_ctrl lc_token_i sampled directly in KMAC main clock (no prim_flop_2sync)
artifact.root_cause: In lc_ctrl_kmac_if, lc_token_i is generated in the clk_io_div4 domain but is captured directly by a single flop (token_q) clocked on clk_main_i, with no two-flop synchronizer or CDC handshake on the io_div4 -> main crossing. Synchronizer depth = 1 (direct capture) where the spec requires depth >= 2 (prim_flop_2sync) for level/single-bit, or a full handshake for multi-bit data.
artifact.fix: Replace the direct capture (token_q <= lc_token_i) with a synchronizer on the crossing. For this 8-bit bus the value must be made coherent: gate/qualify it with a synchronized valid using a CDC handshake, or if it is a stable/gray-coded level, use prim_flop_2sync #(.Width(8)) u_sync (.clk_i(clk_main_i), .rst_ni(rst_ni), .d_i(lc_token_i), .q_o(token_q)). Do not sample lc_token_i directly in clk_main_i.
artifact.impact: Metastability on the io_div4 -> main crossing can propagate corrupted/partially-updated token bits into KMAC, causing functional failures and a security-sensitive data hazard on a life-cycle token. Invisible to zero-delay RTL simulation; first surfaces in gate-level sim or silicon, risking a respin after hundreds of regression hours.

artifact.coverage_status: Bug PROVEN: cdc_report violation (depth 0 vs required 2) + formal CEX. Directed sim PASSES on buggy RTL by design (zero-delay sim blind to CDC) — documented, not fabricated.

proof.note: Invisible to RTL simulation. Found by clock-domain (structural) analysis — not simulation, not functional formal.
